findings: Found 3 new release/package drift issues: current 2.2.0-rc.5 manifests lack matching changelog/status, shared package is omitted from npm verification, and tag-version tooling rewrites quarantined private package versions.

issues_found.title: Current 2.2.0-rc.5 manifests have no matching release notes or README status
issues_found.description: Root and publishable package manifests are at 2.2.0-rc.5, but the top changelog entry is still 2.0.1 GA and README status still says v2.0.0 GA; even the changelog comparison link remains anchored at v2.0.0-rc.4, so a 2.2.0-rc.5 release/tag would ship without matching public release notes or status guidance. Evidence: package.json:4, CHANGELOG.md:8, CHANGELOG.md:1469, README.md:317.
issues_found.fix_direction: Add a 2.2.0-rc.5 changelog entry or Unreleased section with correct compare links, and update README status to the current intended release line before tagging.

issues_found.title: Release checklist omits @fenglimg/fabric-shared from post-publish npm verification
issues_found.description: RELEASING says to verify release packages are visible on npm, but the checklist only runs npm view for @fenglimg/fabric-cli and @fenglimg/fabric-server. @fenglimg/fabric-shared is a versioned published package with files configured, so a failed or stale shared publish would not be caught by the documented gate. Evidence: RELEASING.md:48, RELEASING.md:51, RELEASING.md:52, packages/shared/package.json:2, packages/shared/package.json:3, packages/shared/package.json:67.
issues_found.fix_direction: Add npm view @fenglimg/fabric-shared version to the post-publish checklist and keep the package list generated from the same publishable-workspace source as the version sync gate.

issues_found.title: apply-tag-version rewrites quarantined private package versions unlike sync-versions
issues_found.description: sync-versions explicitly exempts private packages such as the quarantined HTTP package from the release version line, but apply-tag-version collects every packages/* directory and rewrites every manifest version. That means a tag publish mutates @fenglimg/fabric-server-http-experimental from 0.0.0-quarantine to the public tag in CI logs/working tree, contradicting the quarantine marker and the validator's own rule. Evidence: scripts/sync-versions.mjs:62, scripts/sync-versions.mjs:64, scripts/apply-tag-version.mjs:40, scripts/apply-tag-version.mjs:50, scripts/apply-tag-version.mjs:53, packages/server-http-experimental/package.json:3, packages/server-http-experimental/package.json:4.
issues_found.fix_direction: Make apply-tag-version share the same manifest collection/filter as sync-versions, skipping private packages or at least preserving 0.0.0-quarantine for the quarantined package.
